Configuration

Almost all of Merlin's runtime behaviour is controlled through environment variables in the .env file. The config/ directory contains PHP files that read those variables using the env() helper; application code then reads settings through the config() helper rather than touching .env directly. Additional user-editable settings are stored in the database and managed through the Settings pages in the UI.

Environment variables

Copy .env.example to .env and fill in the values for your environment. The sections below describe the most important variables by category.

Database

VariableExample / defaultNotes
DB_CONNECTIONmysqlUse mysql for both MariaDB and MySQL
DB_DATABASEmerlinDatabase name
DB_USERNAMEDatabase user
DB_PASSWORDDatabase password

MariaDB is the preferred database. MySQL is also supported. SQLite is used for automated tests only.

Anthropic (Claude) API

VariableDefault / exampleNotes
ANTHROPIC_API_KEYRequired for invoice extraction
ANTHROPIC_MODELclaude-sonnet-4-6Primary (strong) model — used as the fallback when fast-model output is unsatisfactory
ANTHROPIC_MODEL_FASTclaude-haiku-4-5-20251001Fast model — tried first on every invoice
ANTHROPIC_MODEL_BACKUPclaude-opus-4-8Backup model — used by the health ladder when primary models are unavailable
ANTHROPIC_ALERT_RECIPIENTSComma-separated email addresses alerted when a model is retired or unreachable
ANTHROPIC_MODEL_DOWN_TTL3600Seconds to circuit-break a model that returns a 404 not-found error

Use model aliases (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6) rather than dated snapshot IDs wherever possible. Dated IDs become unavailable when Anthropic retires them, triggering the model health ladder. See Model health and fallback below.

Exchange rates

VariableNotes
EXCHANGERATE_API_KEYAPI key from exchangerate-api.com; required for foreign-currency invoices

Exchange rates are cached for 24 hours (86 400 seconds). If the key is missing or the API is unreachable, foreign-currency conversion will fail. See Troubleshooting if rates stop updating.

Invoice watch folder

VariableDefaultNotes
INVOICE_WATCH_DIRstorage/app/invoice-watchFolder polled by the invoices:watch command
INVOICE_WATCH_INTERVAL10Polling interval in seconds
MAGIKA_BINARYmagikaPath to the Magika binary; only needed if it is not on the system path

The watch folder is an alternative to manual upload: drop invoice files into the folder and php artisan invoices:watch will pick them up automatically on each polling interval.

Queue

VariableDevelopment defaultNotes
QUEUE_CONNECTIONsyncSet to sync in local development; use database or another driver in production

With QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync, queued jobs run immediately in the same process with no worker needed. This is the correct setting for local development. If you switch to the database driver without running a queue worker, jobs will be silently queued but never processed. See Troubleshooting — Queued jobs never run.

Configuration files

In addition to environment variables, Merlin ships with several PHP configuration files in the config/ directory:

FileWhat it holds
config/documents.phpInvoice watch folder path and interval, Magika binary path, document type definitions (prefixes, default statuses), and the tax-exempt rate constant
config/currency.phpExchange rate API key and cache TTL (86 400 s)
config/party.phpBusiness types, relationship types (supplier, client), contact roles, and address types
config/paperdoc.phpSettings for the paperdoc document-parsing library
config/nettmail.phpNETTMAIL_* driver and SMTP/provider settings; nettmail.layout is set to layout.app so NettMail admin pages render inside Merlin's own layout

Business-editable values such as default tax rates, confidence thresholds, and default accounts are stored in the database via the Settings system rather than in these files. See Settings and the individual settings pages under Administration.

Model health and fallback

Merlin has two distinct mechanisms for handling Claude model problems. Do not confuse them — they address different failure modes.

Quality fallback (output-based). On every invoice extraction, Merlin first calls the fast model (ANTHROPIC_MODEL_FAST, typically Haiku). If the response is invalid JSON, the extracted line totals do not reconcile to the invoice total, or the confidence score is below fallback_confidence (default 0.80, configurable in Settings › Purchasing), the entire extraction is retried using the strong model (ANTHROPIC_MODEL, typically Sonnet). This is a quality control mechanism that runs on every invoice and is completely independent of model availability.

Model retirement ladder (availability-based). The ModelHealthService handles the case where a model ID has been retired by Anthropic and returns a 404 not_found_error. When this happens:

  1. The failing model is marked as unavailable in the cache for ANTHROPIC_MODEL_DOWN_TTL seconds (circuit breaker)
  2. The addresses in ANTHROPIC_ALERT_RECIPIENTS receive a one-time alert email
  3. The call escalates to the next available rung: Haiku → Sonnet → Opus

The models:health-check Artisan command probes all three model IDs daily at 05:30 and sends alerts proactively if any are unreachable. To fix a retired model, update the relevant ANTHROPIC_MODEL* variable in .env to a current model alias.
